content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
September 5,
1996
Davis W.
Canpbe11
1526 8th Avenue
Sacramento,
CA 95818
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-96-251
Dear Mr.
Campbell:
You have asked for advice regarding your filing obligation as
a candidate under the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
QUESTION
Will filing Form 490 for the period covering 1/1/96
— 6/30/96
with the Registrars of Voters for Yolo and Sacramento counties
correct previous filing errors.
CONCLUSION
Yes.
You must file Form 490 for the first half of 1996 with
the Registrars of Voters for both Yolo and Sacramento counties.
Doing this will put you back on track in regard to your filing
obligations.
FACTS
You are an appointed member of the Los Rios Community College
District Board of Trustees and you reside in Yolo County.
You are
seeking re—election to your seat on the board on the November 5,
1996 ballot.
The district encompasses Yolo and Sacramento
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
.
All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 3 STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


Campbell,
Davis
Our File No.
A—96—251
Page
2
Counties with the majority of registered voters in Sacramento
County.
On June 24,
1996, you filed Forms 501,
502 and 410.
You were
then advised to file Form 470 which you submitted to Yolo County
on July 31,
1996,
and to Sacramento County on August 1,
1996.
You
have also filed the Form 470-Supplement with Yolo County,
Sacramento County, the Secretary of State, and your opponent in
the election.
You are prepared to file Form 490 for the first half of 1996.
ANALYSIS
Section 84206 states that:
a)
The commission shall provide by regulation for a
short form for filing reports required by this article
for candidates or officeholders who receive
contributions of less than one thousand dollars
($1, 000),
and who make expenditures of less than one
thousand dollars ($1,000),
in a calendar year.
*
*
*
c)
Every candidate or officeholder who has filed a
short form pursuant to subdivision (a),
and who
thereafter receives contributions or makes expenditures
totaling one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more in a
calendar year, shall send written notification to the
Secretary of State, the local filing officer,
and each
candidate contending for the same office within 48 hours
of receiving or expending a total of one thousand
dollars
($1,000).
The written notification shall revoke
the previously filed short form statement.
The Form 470 is the short form authorized by Section 84206.
The Form 470-Supplement functions as the written notification
mentioned in Section 84206(c).
Because you intended to raise and spend $1,000 or more before
the end of the year,
you should have filed Form 490
(Officeholder/
Candidate and Controlled Committee Campaign Statement
- Long Form)
rather than Form 470.
Section 84200 provides that candidates
shall file semiannual statements each year no later than July 31
for the period ending June 30 and no later than January 31 for the
period ending December 31.
Section 84215 provides that a filer such as yourself must
file with the clerk of the county of both the candidate!
officeholder’s county of domicile and the county with the largest
number of registered voters within a multi—county jurisdiction.
In both Yolo and Sacramento counties it is the office of the
registrar of voters which serves as the filing officer under the
Political Reform Act of 1974.


Campbell,
Davis
Our File No. A-96-251
Page
3
If you have additional questions, please contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
By:
w4ne .
Imberi
Po”litical Reform Consultant

correct previous filing errors.

the Registrars of Voters for both Yolo and Sacramento counties.
Doing this will put you back on track in regard to your filing
obligations.

QUESTION: Will filing Form 490 for the period covering 1/1/96
— 6/30/96
with the Registrars of Voters for Yolo and Sacramento counties
correct previous filing errors.

CONCLUSION: Yes.
You must file Form 490 for the first half of 1996 with
the Registrars of Voters for both Yolo and Sacramento counties.
Doing this will put you back on track in regard to your filing
obligations.
